cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down the problem step by step. Anthony went to the amusement park today. We need to determine between what times he could have gone, based on the given information. The options are (A) 9pm to 10pm, (B) 9am to 2pm, (C) 6am to 8am, and (D) 2pm to 9pm. Let's analyze the information provided: 1. Anthony woke up at 6am. 2. Leslie saw Anthony reading at the library from 6am to 8am. 3. Jennifer saw Anthony working out at the gym from 8am to 9am. 4. Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies at a bakery from 2pm to 9pm. 5. William saw Anthony buying a bike at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. 6. The amusement park was closed after 10pm. Now, let's determine Anthony's schedule: - From 6am to 8am, Anthony was reading at the library (confirmed by Leslie). - From 8am to 9am, Anthony was working out at the gym (confirmed by Jennifer). - From 2pm to 9pm, Anthony was buying cookies at a bakery (confirmed by Anthony himself). - From 9pm to 10pm, Anthony was buying a bike at the bike shop (confirmed by William). The amusement park was closed after 10pm, so the latest Anthony could have gone is just before 10pm. Now, let's examine the time slots when Anthony was not doing any of these activities.
